commands.review_summary:
This branch bounds `sessions_history`, preserves `includeTools`, adds `tools.sessionStatus.details`, updates docs/prompt snapshots/tests, and compacts Codex context/trajectory diagnostics.

PR surface: Source +224, Tests +329, Docs +34. Total +587 across 24 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible for the review blockers: the PR-head source shows the hardcoded Codex mission anchor and shows `chat.history` being limited before the added tool-message filter. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the PR has blocking source findings, a security-sensitive prompt-context issue, compatibility/product choices, and insufficient contributor real-behavior proof.

target.title: fix: bound session history and compact status opt-in
target.body:
## Summary

This keeps shipped session-tool contracts compatible while reducing context-bloat risk from recovery/debug replay.

- Keep `sessions_history.includeTools` available for existing callers.
- Default `sessions_history` returns the last 10 sanitized non-tool turns.
- Add a stricter byte cap for `includeTools: true` replay, while preserving the existing non-tool history cap.
- Keep full `session_status` structured details as the default.
- Add `tools.sessionStatus.details="compact"` as an opt-in smaller details mode.
- Update model-facing guidance, docs, tool display metadata, tests, and prompt snapshots.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `sessions_history` could replay too much session data into model context, and `session_status` duplicated status text in structured details without an opt-in compact mode.
- Real environment tested: Installed local OpenClaw runtime, version `2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)`, patched with the same compatibility behavior and restarted through the local gateway.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran live `openclaw gateway call tools.invoke` probes for `sessions_history` with and without `includeTools`, ran `session_status`, then checked gateway debug state.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```console
$ openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)

$ openclaw gateway call tools.invoke --json --params '{"name":"sessions_history","args":{"sessionKey":"<redacted-session>","includeTools":true}}' | jq '{ok, bytes:.output.details.bytes, messageCount:(.output.details.messages|length), hasToolRole:any(.output.details.messages[]; .role=="toolResult" or .role=="tool"), hasToolBlock:any(.output.details.messages[]; (.content // []) as $c | ($c|type=="array") and any($c[]; (.type=="toolCall" or .type=="toolUse" or .type=="toolCallDelta")))}'
{
  "ok": true,
  "bytes": 10785,
  "messageCount": 10,
  "hasToolRole": true,
  "hasToolBlock": true
}

$ openclaw gateway call tools.invoke --json --params '{"name":"sessions_history","args":{"sessionKey":"<redacted-session>"}}' | jq '{ok, bytes:.output.details.bytes, messageCount:(.output.details.messages|length), hasToolRole:any(.output.details.messages[]; .role=="toolResult" or .role=="tool"), hasToolBlock:any(.output.details.messages[]; (.content // []) as $c | ($c|type=="array") and any($c[]; (.type=="toolCall" or .type=="toolUse" or .type=="toolCallDelta")))}'
{
  "ok": true,
  "bytes": 3663,
  "messageCount": 5,
  "hasToolRole": false,
  "hasToolBlock": false
}

$ openclaw gateway call tools.invoke --json --params '{"name":"session_status","args":{"sessionKey":"<redacted-session>"}}' | jq '{ok, hasDetailsStatusText:(.output.details|has("statusText")), textChars:(.output.content[0].text|length), detailsStatusTextChars:(.output.details.statusText|length // null)}'
{
  "ok": true,
  "hasDetailsStatusText": true,
  "textChars": 601,
  "detailsStatusTextChars": 601
}

$ curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:<local-port>/api/debug | jq '{gatewayConnected,pendingRequests,activeSessionKey}'
{
  "gatewayConnected": true,
  "pendingRequests": 0,
  "activeSessionKey": null
}
```

- Observed result after fix: `includeTools` remains available for existing callers, default history excludes tool rows/blocks, tool-inclusive replay is bounded, and default `session_status` keeps full details while compact details are opt-in.
- What was not tested: Upstream maintainer production deployment and every channel-specific UI surface were not covered by this local runtime proof.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The copied live output is redacted to remove local session identifiers and host-specific endpoint details.

## Validation

- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/agents/tool-display.test.ts src/config/config.tools-alsoAllow.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`
- `git diff --check`

## Notes

The current shape intentionally favors backward compatibility over removing shipped fields. Tool-inclusive history remains available, but bounded and sanitized for recovery/debug use rather than unbounded transcript replay.
